_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to write with clearly formed joined letters that are becoming faster and more automatic.

_source.w: We are learning to write with clearly formed joined letters that are becoming faster and more automatic.
y: Fluent, automatic handwriting means we can focus our attention on our ideas rather than on forming letters.
Speed with legibility is the goal — writing that is both quick and easy to read.
This automaticity is built through consistent, regular practice.
eg: We practise joined writing daily to build speed and fluency.
We focus on writing smoothly and consistently, keeping our letters evenly sized.
We write longer passages without stopping to think about how to form each letter.
We compare our handwriting from the beginning and end of term to see our improvement.
We use joined writing consistently across all our school work.
We develop a comfortable, personal style of joined writing that is both legible and efficient.
